question: What is the definition of the abbreviation NPM?

nuscale_ch01_0035.title: Fuel Handling and Reactor Maintenance Areas
nuscale_ch01_0035.summary: The fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas are located at the west end of the Reactor Building (RXB) and include the spent fuel pool (SFP), refueling pool, and dry dock, with dedicated spaces and equipment for fuel handling and maintenance operations. These areas are designed for safe transport, inspection, and temporary storage of new and spent fuel assemblies, while also providing radiation protection for personnel during operations and maintenance activities.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It lists and describes various symbols representing sample probes, pressure and temperature sensors, flow measurement devices, and other auxiliary instrumentation components, ensuring standardized interpretation of the diagrams.

nuscale_ch01_0036.title: Refueling Operations
nuscale_ch01_0036.summary: Refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module (NPM) are conducted independently from other modules. The process involves moving the NPM from its operating bay to the refueling pool, disassembling it for core refueling and maintenance, and then reassembling and returning it to service after inspections and tests are completed.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis diagrams. It describes the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols relevant to system status and control, ensuring clear interpretation of the diagrams. Additionally, it includes notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions, such as vent and drain representations.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides a summary of the NuScale plant’s overall design, including its main systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, and waste management. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, clarifies how representative information is used for context, and notes that each COL applicant must reference and supplement the NuScale FSAR with site-specific details.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. Tables and figures are numbered according to their section, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in accordance with regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section explains that the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report includes simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It references tables and figures listing and explaining the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and identification of text, tables, figures, and page numbering within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR). It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure in accordance with regulations, and a separate public version is provided with such material removed.

edges.description: Section B provides the legend for abbreviations and symbols used in diagrams, which is necessary to interpret terms like NPM that may appear in the general plant description of Section A.
Section B uses the abbreviation 'NPM' in context, while Section A is expected to define such abbreviations, but does not provide the definition for 'NPM' in the provided excerpt.

Section B uses the abbreviation 'NPM' multiple times without defining it, while Section A is titled 'Acronyms and Abbreviations' but does not provide the definition for 'NPM', indicating that Section A is intended to specify such definitions for terms like 'NPM' used in Section B.
Section B provides diagram legends that may include abbreviations such as NPM, which Section A references as part of the figures used to clarify design information.
Section A provides a general description of the plant but does not define abbreviations, while Section B is likely to contain the definition of NPM as it covers acronyms and abbreviations.
Section A provides a general overview of plant systems, while Section B offers detailed drawings that visually clarify and supplement the descriptions found in Section A.

Section A and Section B both discuss the numbering and identification of tables and figures within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, with Section A referencing Table 1 and Section B explaining the general convention for table and figure numbering.
